content: Arrange in decreasing order, the energy of $2 s$ orbital in the following atoms $\mathrm{H}, \mathrm{Li}, \mathrm{Na}$ and K .
explanation:
The correct order (decreasing) of energy of 2 s orbital for given atom is

$$E_{2 s}(\mathrm{H})>E_{2 s}(\mathrm{Li})>E_{2 s}(\mathrm{Na})>E_{2 s}(\mathrm{~K})$$

The reason is that as atomic number (nuclear charge) increases, all the orbitals are pulled closer to the nucleus. Closes in an orbital to the nucleus, less is its energy.

content: The number of radial nodes in 3s and 2p respectiely are
explanation:
Number of radial nodes $=n-I-1$

For $3 s$, radial nodes $=3-0-1=2$

For $2 p$, radial nodes $=2-1-1=0$

Hence, the number of radial nodes are 2, and 0 respectively.

content: The uncertainty in the momentum of an electron is $$2.0 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$$. The uncertainty in its position will be
explanation:
According to Heisenberg uncentainty principle,

$$\Delta x \times \Delta p \geq \frac{h}{4 \pi}$$

Where, $$\Delta x=$$ uncertainty in position

$$\Delta p=$$ uncertainty in momentum

$$\begin{aligned} & 2.0 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{kgms}^{-1} \\ \therefore & \Delta x \times 2.0 \times 10^{-8} \geq \frac{6.62 \times 10^{-34}}{4 \times 3.14} \\ & \Delta x \geq \frac{6.62 \times 10^{-34}}{4 \times 3.14 \times 2.0 \times 10^{-8}} \geq 2.63 \times 10^{-27} \mathrm{~m} \end{aligned}$$

content: In which of the following photoelectric effect is observed?
explanation: Only the group I elements of periodic table exhibit the photoelectric effect because they are highly electropositive metals and have low ionisation enthalpy. Hence, only Cs shows photoelectric effect among the given options.

content: An electron in H-atom in its ground state absorbs 1.50 times as much as energy as the minimum required for its escape $$(13.6 \mathrm{~eV})$$ from the atom. Thus, KE given to emitted electron is
explanation:
Energy in ground state $$=13.6 \mathrm{~eV}$$

Energy absorbed $$=1.5 \times 13.6 \mathrm{~eV}=20.4 \mathrm{~eV}$$

Energy in higher level $$=13.6+20.4=34.0 \mathrm{~eV}$$

$$\therefore$$ Energy emitted $$=34.0-13.6=20.4 \mathrm{~eV}$$

$$\therefore \mathrm{KE}$$ to the emitted electron $$=20.4 \mathrm{~eV}$$

content: What is the total number of electrons that can have the values $$n=2, l=1, s=1 / 2$$ in the electronic configuration $$1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^3$$ ?
explanation: According to Pauli's exclusion principle "No two electrons in an atoms can have the same set of four quantum numbers.

content: Calculate the wavelength associated with an electron moving with a velocity of $$10^6 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$$ (mass of electron $$=9.1 \times 10^{-31} \mathrm{~kg}, h=6.6 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^2 \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$$ )
Which of the following pairs is not correctly matched?

options.content:
Hund's rule
In orbitals of equivalent energy electron spins remain unpaired if possible
Pauli's exclusion principle No two electrons can have all the four quantum numbers identical

Zeeman effect
The effect of magnetic field on the automatic spectra

Uncertainty principle
It is impossible to determine the position of an electron

explanation: As per uncertainty principle "It is impossible to determine the position and momentum of a moving electrons in an atoms with accuracy or certainty.

content: The orbital diagram in which Aufbau principle is violated is
explanation:
In option (c), Aufbau principle is violated. According to this rule, the orbital with lower value of $$n$$, i.e., 25 will be filled first completely.

In option (a) "Hund's rule of maximum multiplicity" is violated.

According to this rule, the pairing of electrons in orbitals of a subshell does not take place until all the orbitals of a subshell are singly occupied.
